campaign.title: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm
campaign.strategy_summary: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm: build habits that bend without breaking. Generate measurable early-adopter acquisition learning from Instagram while preserving a clear separation between landing interest, auth intent, registration, activation, and product usage.

campaign_execution_summary.creative_concept: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm: a July learning cycle showing that habits can recalibrate without treating imperfect days as failure.
narrative_sequence: Start with recognisable friction around streaks and perfect-day plans.
Introduce Innerbloom’s adaptive rhythm as a calmer alternative.
Make the mechanism concrete with Daily Quest, task intensity, dashboard progress, and visible momentum.
Invite early adopters to try the early version and share feedback.
campaign_execution_summary.asset_reuse_summary: Reuses June approved carousel/static visual language and the available Daily Quest screenshot; derivative edits are queued, not produced.

posts.target_audience: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks. This audience is inferred from current positioning and prior hypotheses, not directly validated by audience research.
posts.audience_problem: Feeling punished when a streak breaks; Restarting from zero after busy or low-energy weeks; Plans that assume every day has the same capacity; Wanting visible progress without shame or perfection pressure
posts.hook: Your streak broke. Your progress did not.
posts.caption:
Your streak broke. Your progress did not.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

Start with a rhythm that fits this week: https://innerbloomjourney.org/?utm_source=instagram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=ib_202607&utm_content=post_001&ib_post=001

cta.text: Start with a rhythm that fits this week

posts.hypothesis: Pain/friction posts will produce a higher landing CTA rate than product mechanism posts because the audience problem is easier to recognize than the product solution.
posts.primary_metric: landing_cta_clicked divided by landing page sessions for posts in each pillar, using UTM and ib_post where available
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If one pillar produces at least 2x the CTA rate of the other with at least 10 landing sessions per tested pillar, prioritize that pillar next month; otherwise classify as inconclusive.

posts.target_audience: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks. This audience is inferred from current positioning and prior hypotheses, not directly validated by audience research.
posts.audience_problem: Feeling punished when a streak breaks; Restarting from zero after busy or low-energy weeks; Plans that assume every day has the same capacity; Wanting visible progress without shame or perfection pressure
posts.hook: Perfect-day plans fail real weeks.
posts.caption:
Perfect-day plans fail real weeks.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

See how adaptive habits work: https://innerbloomjourney.org/?utm_source=instagram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=ib_202607&utm_content=post_002&ib_post=002
posts.hypothesis: Carousels will improve scroll/engagement and CTA intent for explanatory concepts compared with static statements.
posts.primary_metric: scroll events and landing_cta_clicked per session by format for UTM-tagged posts
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If carousel posts exceed static posts by 30% or more on CTA per session with comparable sessions, keep carousel for explanatory topics; otherwise reserve carousel for product demos only.

posts.hook: Daily Quest starts with today, not ideal days.
posts.caption:
Daily Quest starts with today, not ideal days.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

posts.hypothesis: Using the available Daily Quest screenshot will make the adaptive habit mechanism more concrete and increase landing CTA clicks versus abstract product mechanism visuals.
posts.primary_metric: landing_cta_clicked per session and dashboard_view event count for product mechanism posts that use screenshots versus reusable abstract visuals
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If screenshot-backed posts show higher CTA per session and at least one downstream dashboard_view event, request more product screenshots next cycle; otherwise reuse screenshots sparingly.

posts.hook: Innerbloom is early. Your feedback matters.
posts.caption:
Innerbloom is early. Your feedback matters.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

Help shape Innerbloom: https://innerbloomjourney.org/?utm_source=instagram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=ib_202607&utm_content=post_008&ib_post=008
posts.hypothesis: Posts that explicitly say the product is early and feedback is welcome will generate more auth_started events per landing session than generic try-it CTAs.
posts.primary_metric: auth_started per landing session for Early-adopter feedback CTA posts
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If feedback CTA posts generate at least 3 auth_started events and outperform non-feedback posts on auth_started per session, keep transparent early-adopter CTAs; otherwise rewrite the CTA proposition.

secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If screenshot-backed posts show higher CTA per session and at least one downstream dashboard_view event, request more product screenshots next cycle; otherwise reuse screenshots sparingly.

posts.target_audience: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks. This audience is inferred from current positioning and prior hypotheses, not directly validated by audience research.
posts.audience_problem: Feeling punished when a streak breaks; Restarting from zero after busy or low-energy weeks; Plans that assume every day has the same capacity; Wanting visible progress without shame or perfection pressure
posts.hook: Missing a day should not become a verdict.
posts.caption:
Missing a day should not become a verdict.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

Start with a rhythm that fits this week: https://innerbloomjourney.org/?utm_source=instagram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=ib_202607&utm_content=post_012&ib_post=012

cta.text: Start with a rhythm that fits this week

posts.hypothesis: Pain/friction posts will produce a higher landing CTA rate than product mechanism posts because the audience problem is easier to recognize than the product solution.
posts.primary_metric: landing_cta_clicked divided by landing page sessions for posts in each pillar, using UTM and ib_post where available
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If one pillar produces at least 2x the CTA rate of the other with at least 10 landing sessions per tested pillar, prioritize that pillar next month; otherwise classify as inconclusive.

posts.hook: Try the dashboard, then tell us what felt unclear.
posts.caption:
Try the dashboard, then tell us what felt unclear.

Innerbloom is for people who want habits that keep direction when the week changes. The point is not to pretend every day has the same energy, stress, or time. It is to make the next step adjustable enough to continue without turning one difficult day into a full restart.

This July campaign is a learning cycle: we are measuring landing interest, CTA clicks, and auth intent separately, without treating product usage as proof of acquisition.

Give feedback after trying the dashboard: https://innerbloomjourney.org/?utm_source=instagram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=ib_202607&utm_content=post_016&ib_post=016

cta.text: Give feedback after trying the dashboard

posts.hypothesis: Posts that explicitly say the product is early and feedback is welcome will generate more auth_started events per landing session than generic try-it CTAs.
posts.primary_metric: auth_started per landing session for Early-adopter feedback CTA posts
secondary_metrics: landing page sessions by UTM post
posts.success_signal: If feedback CTA posts generate at least 3 auth_started events and outperform non-feedback posts on auth_started per session, keep transparent early-adopter CTAs; otherwise rewrite the CTA proposition.

transformation_instructions: Create a square Instagram carousel visual for Your streak broke. Your progress did not..
Use only the listed source asset(s); crop or frame to the central message/product area.
Add restrained Innerbloom-style text overlays/callouts matching the post hook; keep typography readable on mobile.
For product screenshots, highlight existing visible Daily Quest/task-selection UI only; do not alter product data or invent screens.
text_content: Your streak broke. Your progress did not.
truthfulness_constraints: Do not change visible product data, UI labels, task names, progress, or capability shown in screenshots.
Do not imply guaranteed results, medical benefits, published status, or measured performance.
Do not claim the derivative asset already exists.
acceptance_criteria: 1080x1080 PNG is legible on mobile.
Source asset remains recognizable and unmodified in factual UI content.
Any overlay supports the hook without adding unsupported claims.
Alt text can accurately describe the final visual.
